name: Health insurance (US individual / family)
summary: Individual and family health insurance — high complexity, high welfare-delta per household.

evidence.claim: Metal tier differences (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum) reflect the actuarial split of cost sharing: Bronze covers ~60% of costs, Silver ~70%, Gold ~80%, Platinum ~90%.

Cost-sharing reduction (CSR) subsidies are only available with SILVER plans on the ACA marketplace for qualifying income levels — an asymmetry that rewards picking Silver over Bronze for many middle-income households.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Every ACA-compliant plan covers the 10 essential health benefits. 'Comprehensive' is a marketing feel-word; the real differences are in cost-sharing, network, and formulary.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What are the specific deductible, out-of-pocket max, and network adequacy differences?

confabulationPatterns.reality: High-deductible plans have low premiums but can cost $10,000+ in a bad year. Total-cost analysis requires expected claims: premium + expected cost-sharing = actual annual cost (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the user computed total expected annual cost, not just premium?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Not always optimal. Middle-income households with qualifying subsidy income get CSR only on Silver — making Silver the lowest-cost option (E2). Check subsidy eligibility before assuming higher metal = better.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the user checked CSR subsidy eligibility? If yes and income qualifies, Silver is often optimal despite worse headline tier.

counterfeitSignals.signal: 'Short-term' plan marketed as ACA-compliant — they are NOT
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as dangerous; short-term plans exclude pre-existing conditions and have annual caps

compatibilityQuestions: Do you need specific doctors / hospitals? Check in-network status.
Any prescription drugs? Check the formulary — same drug can cost $0 or $500/mo across plans.
Are you or family eligible for CSR subsidies? That determines whether Silver is your optimal tier.
Planned surgeries or pregnancy? Higher metal tiers reduce out-of-pocket.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Out-of-network surprise bills (reduced by No Surprises Act, 2022)
